visual_goal: Clean premium optical-bench teaching scene showing a thin white incident ray refracting through a transparent prism and dispersing into separated red, green, and blue screen dots with sparse attached labels and small formulas.
critic_priorities: preserve the left-to-right optical bench layout
keep prism transparency and bright glass edges visible
make thin rays, normals, and angle arcs readable without arrow clutter
show red bends least, green middle, and blue most
keep formulas small and away from the prism and ray intersections

must_have: glass_prism, incident_white_ray, projection_screen, and RGB exit rays remain visible
surface_normal_entry, surface_normal_exit, theta_i, and theta_t are readable when introduced
Snell formula and refractive-index inequality appear small and secondary
red, green, and blue rays land as separated screen dots
left-to-right order is white ray, prism, colored rays, screen
must_avoid: opaque triangle replacing the transparent glass prism
thick laser bars, cone arrowheads, or sci-fi beam effects
large classroom panels covering the prism or screen
unrelated scenery such as rain, vehicles, water glasses, or museum lasers
aesthetic_goals: dark matte bench with subtle grid ticks
light cyan glass tint with bright beveled prism edges
sparse labels attached directly to geometry
blocking_conditions: required prism, incident ray, colored rays, or projection screen is missing
dispersion order is wrong or the screen dots are not separated
formula overlays obscure the central refraction geometry
rays are too thick or cluttered to read
the scene becomes a generic black void or unrelated cinematic environment

repair_policy: replace any thick beam or arrow primitive with a thin readable ray polyline
keep labels short and attached beside their referenced ray, normal, angle, or screen
move formulas to the lower bench edge or a clear side area if they overlap the prism
restore transparent cyan glass material with bright edges if the prism becomes opaque
separate red, green, and blue ray paths and their screen dots if they merge
remove unrelated props, decorative shapes, and oversized classroom panels
spatial_relationships: incident_white_ray enters from the left toward glass_prism
surface_normal_entry is perpendicular to the left prism face at the entry point
surface_normal_exit is perpendicular to the right prism face at the exit point
spectrum_red_ray bends least, spectrum_green_ray stays between, and spectrum_blue_ray bends most
projection_screen stands to the right of glass_prism
red_screen_dot, green_screen_dot, and blue_screen_dot sit separated on projection_screen at the ray endpoints
forbidden_abstractions: glass_prism replaced by a flat opaque triangle
projection_screen replaced by a blank glowing rectangle with no RGB dots
RGB rays collapsed into one white or rainbow smear
surface normals shown as generic axes unrelated to prism faces
formula blocks floating over the center of the prism
optical bench removed into a pure black void
decorative abstract particles substituted for the actual ray geometry
